goal: Map whether the runtime patch permit-consume tower is mounted or externally imported before any deletion task is approved.
scope_out: Do not delete runtime patch permit-consume files in BPK-232.
Do not change source, tests, routes, env, DB, deploy, provider, write, runtime, activation, package, or workflow behavior.
claims: The runtime patch permit-consume cluster is not mounted in builder/src/server.ts.
The cluster has no external source importers and no external test importers.
The cluster is separate from the retained permitApply write path.
Any actual deletion should be a later human-approved task.
assumptions: Static import and route scans are sufficient for a decision map, but a later deletion task still needs typecheck and full tests as backstops.
Historical contracts and review packets may keep references as audit history unless a later task decides otherwise.
dod: docs/RUNTIME-PATCH-PERMIT-CONSUME-DEPENDENCY-MAP.md records file counts, route findings, import findings, and the next-task guardrails.
BPK-232 does not modify builder/src or builder/tests.
Task-lock verify, content checks, and diff-check pass.
required_commands: rg -n "Source files: 41|External source importers: 0|route mounts.*: 0|BPK-233" docs/RUNTIME-PATCH-PERMIT-CONSUME-DEPENDENCY-MAP.md review-packets/BPK-232.md STATE.md docs/SESSION-LOG.md
stop_conditions: A source, test, package, workflow, env, DB, deploy, runtime activation, or deletion change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-231 added local emergency stop and daily provider-token cap at provider and permitApply entry points. BPK-229 recommended a dependency graph for the runtime patch permit-consume tower before any deletion.
